title: TEP Fund 2026 LP subscription agreement

steps.label: BENEFICIAL OWNERSHIP FORM AND CERTIFICATION FOR TRUSTS, CORPORATIONS, LLCs, AND LPs
EXHIBIT A - ACCREDITED INVESTOR VERIFICATION [For BD/RIA Use Only]

items.title: All parties (account holders) have signed and dated where required, including the W-9 form

The Primary Account Holder information has been completed for all account types (including date of birth, social security number, zip code and telephone numbers). COPY OF VALID DRIVERS LICENSE IS REQUIRED.
Signature page section has been completed
Broker Information section has been completed and signed

Accredited investor verification: (i) provided financial statements/appraisals within 3 months AND (ii) provided consumer credit report within 3 months AND (iii) certified all liabilities disclosed; OR (iv) provided written confirmation from authorized person
If subscription is for a Trust: included copy of title page, authorized individual page and signature page of Trust Agreement (or N/A)
If subscription is for IRA/Qualified/Custodial Account: IRA Information completed, sent to Custodian for signature, Custodian provided Corporate Charter and W-9 (or N/A)
If subscription is for corporate/LLC/partnership: included Articles or Certificate of Partnership/Formation/Incorporation and W-9 (or N/A)
If there are third party signatures: included Powers of Attorney authorizing such signatures (or N/A)
Subscription Agreement Completed

items.title: The Investor hereby adopts, confirms, and agrees to all of the covenants, representations and warranties set forth in this Subscription Agreement.

The Investor hereby certifies that the Investor is an "accredited investor" as such term is defined under Regulation D promulgated under the Securities Act pursuant to the following representations (check all that apply):

options.label: The Investor has an individual net worth, or the Investor's spouse and the Investor has a combined net worth, in excess of $1,000,000.

The Investor has individual income (exclusive of any income attributable to the Investor's spouse) of more than $200,000 in each of the past two years, or joint income with the Investor's spouse of more than $300,000 in each of those years, and the Investor reasonably expects to reach the same income level in the current year.

The Investor is an individual retirement account or Keogh plan, the individual for whose benefit the investment in the Partnership is being made has directed such investment, and such individual is an accredited investor because such individual has a net worth or income as described above.

The Investor is a bank as defined in Section 3(a)(2) of the Securities Act or any savings and loan association or other institution as defined in Section 3(a)(5)(A) of the Securities Act, whether acting in its individual or fiduciary capacity.
The Investor is a broker-dealer registered pursuant to Section 15 of the Exchange Act.
The Investor is an insurance company as defined in Section 2(a)(13) of the Securities Act.
The Investor is an investment adviser registered pursuant to Section 203 of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940, as amended, or registered pursuant to the laws of a state.
The Investor is an investment company registered under the Investment Company Act of 1940, as amended.
The Investor is a director or executive officer of the Partnership.

The Investor is a natural person who holds, in good standing, one of the following professional certifications or licenses: the General Securities Representative license (Series 7); the Private Securities Offerings Representative license (Series 82); or the Investment Adviser Representative license (Series 65).

The Investor is a natural person who is a "knowledgeable employee," as defined in Rule 3c-5(a)(4) under the Investment Company Act, of the Partnership, where the Partnership would be an investment company, as defined in section 3 of the Investment Company Act, but for the exclusion provided by either section 3(c)(1) or section 3(c)(7) of the Investment Company Act.
Small business investment company licensed by the Small Business Administration under Section 301(c) or (d) of the Small Business Investment Act of 1958
Rural Business Investment Company as defined in section 384A of the Consolidated Farm and Rural Development Act

Plan established and maintained by a state, its political subdivisions, or any agency or instrumentality of a state or its political subdivisions, for the benefit of its employees, if the plan has total assets in excess of $5 million

Employee benefit plan within the meaning of ERISA if the decision to invest in the Interests is made by a plan fiduciary (bank, savings and loan, insurance company or registered investment advisor), or if the employee benefit plan has total assets in excess of $5 million or, if a self-directed plan, with investment decisions made solely by persons that are accredited investors

Family office with assets under management in excess of $5 million, not formed for the specific purpose of acquiring the Interests, with a person directing the prospective investment who has such knowledge and experience in financial and business matters
Family client of a family office meeting the requirements listed above and whose prospective investment in the Partnership is directed by that family office

Trust with total assets in excess of $5,000,000, not formed for the specific purpose of acquiring Interests, and its purchase is directed by a person who has such knowledge and experience in financial and business matters
Trust having as its trustee or co-trustee a bank, savings and loan association, or another institution which makes or participates in the investment decision
Revocable trust which may be amended or revoked at any time by the grantors thereof and all of the grantors are accredited investors

The Investor is not a Natural Person and (check all that apply)

options.label: is an entity, acting for its own account or the accounts of other "qualified purchasers," that in the aggregate owns and invests on a discretionary basis, not less than $25,000,000 in "Investments."
is a "family company" that owns not less than $5,000,000 in "Investments."
is an entity (other than a trust), each of the beneficial owners of which is a "qualified purchaser."

is a trust (i) that is not Family Company owning at least $5,000,000 in "Investments," (ii) that was not formed for the specific purpose of acquiring the Interest, (iii) for which each trustee (or other person authorized to make decisions with respect to the trust) and (iv) for which each grantor (or other person who has contributed assets to the trust) of which are "qualified purchasers."

is a "qualified institutional buyer" (as defined in paragraph (a) of Rule 144A under the Securities Act) that is (i) not an entity covered by the bullet point immediately below and (ii) acting for its own account, the account of another "qualified institutional buyer," or the account of a "qualified purchaser."

is a "qualified institutional buyer" (as defined in paragraph (a) of Rule 144A under the Securities Act) that is an entity that either (i) is a dealer described in paragraph (a)(1)(ii) of Rule 144A that owns and invests on a discretionary basis at least $25,000,000 in securities of issuers that are not affiliated persons of the dealer; or (ii) a plan described in paragraph (a)(1)(i)(D) or (a)(1)(i)(E) of Rule 144A, or a trust fund described in paragraph (a)(1)(i)(F) of Rule 144A that holds the assets of such a plan, the investment decisions of which are made solely by the fiduciary, trustee or sponsor of such plan

The Investor is an "employee benefit plan" (within the meaning of Section 3(3) of ERISA) subject to Title I of ERISA.
